01_initial_request.user_intent: Assess neurobehavioural changes in a mouse model of extinguished chronic colitis and explore associated changes along the gut-brain axis
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Reduced spontaneous activity and upregulation of inflammatory gene expression

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) are often associated with psychological comorbidities such as fatigue, with up to 47% of patients reporting fatigue despite disease remission. [Gap] There is a need to assess neurobehavioural changes in a mouse model of extinguished chronic colitis to mimic quiescent IBD. [Decision] To achieve a model of quiescent IBD, chose to use DSS administration to perform repeated administration of 2% dextran sulphate sodium followed by a recovery period on C57BL/6 mice, expecting to obtain chronic inflammation with minimal residual intestinal symptoms.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Chronic inflammation with minimal residual intestinal symptoms (median DAI of 0.5 vs 0 in control, P<0.0001) after 3 weeks of recovery.

references.citation: M. Truyens et al., Abstract citation ID: jjac190.0144
references.relevance: Describes the induction of chronic colitis using DSS to mimic quiescent IBD.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] A mouse model of extinguished chronic colitis has been established. [Gap] Neurobehavioural changes in this model are currently unknown. [Decision] To assess neurobehavioural changes, chose to use Behavioural testing to perform testing at baseline and after the recovery period on mice, expecting to obtain activity data compared with control mice.

parameters.timepoints: baseline and after the recovery period

02_agent_trajectory.observation: cDSS mice exhibited significantly reduced spontaneous activity compared with baseline (P=0.0084) and controls (P=0.0186).

references.citation: M. Truyens et al., Abstract citation ID: jjac190.0144
references.relevance: Reports the behavioral testing results indicating fatigue-like behaviour.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Behavioural changes indicative of fatigue have been observed. [Gap] Molecular changes along the gut-brain axis associated with these changes are unexplored. [Decision] To explore associated changes along the gut-brain axis, chose to use RNA sequencing to perform sequencing of the distal colon and choroid plexus, expecting to obtain gene expression data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Expression data generated for distal colon and choroid plexus.

references.citation: M. Truyens et al., Abstract citation ID: jjac190.0144
references.relevance: Describes the RNA sequencing methodology on specific tissues.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] RNA sequencing expression data is available. [Gap] Specific co-expressed gene modules differing between control and cDSS mice are unidentified. [Decision] To identify gene modules, chose to use WGCNA to perform weighted correlation network analysis on the colon and the choroid plexus expression data, expecting to obtain a consensus module of co-expressed genes.

parameters.input_data: colon and the choroid plexus expression data

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Revealed a consensus module of 123 co-expressed genes that differed significantly between control and cDSS mice (P=0.00001721), implied in pathways of neutrophil and complement activation.

references.citation: M. Truyens et al., Abstract citation ID: jjac190.0144
references.relevance: Details the application of WGCNA and the resulting gene module.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] WGCNA analysis identified a consensus module of genes. [Gap] Expression levels of specific inflammatory genes in different brain regions need confirmation. [Decision] To confirm the expression levels of genes identified in the WGCNA analysis, chose to use qPCR to perform quantitative polymerase chain reaction on frontal cortex, hippocampus and choroid plexus, expecting to obtain confirmation of inflammatory genes Lcn2, TNFa, S100A8 and S100A9.

parameters.regions: frontal cortex, hippocampus and choroid plexus

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Confirmed upregulation of inflammatory genes Lcn2, TNFa, S100A8 and S100A9.

references.citation: M. Truyens et al., Abstract citation ID: jjac190.0144
references.relevance: Confirms the upregulation of specific neuroinflammation markers via qPCR.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Inflammatory gene upregulation suggests neuroinflammation. [Gap] The status of microglia activation and morphology is unknown. [Decision] To assess the number of microglia and their morphology, chose to use Iba1 immunofluorescence to perform assessment in a second experiment including acute colitis on prefrontal cortex and hippocampus, expecting to obtain microglia activation data.

parameters.groups: control, acute colitis (1 week 2% DSS), chronic colitis recovered

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Acute colitis associated with activation of microglia in prefrontal cortex and hippocampus, persisting in the prefrontal cortex of mice that recovered from chronic colitis.

references.citation: M. Truyens et al., Abstract citation ID: jjac190.0144
references.relevance: Describes the immunofluorescence assessment of microglia.

spontaneous_activity_reduction.interpretation: Significantly reduced spontaneous activity in cDSS mice compared with controls, indicating fatigue-like behaviour.

gene_expression_upregulation.interpretation: Confirmed upregulation of inflammatory genes Lcn2, TNFa, S100A8 and S100A9 in brain regions.

wgcna_module_significance.interpretation: Consensus module of 123 co-expressed genes differed significantly between control and cDSS mice.

microglia_activation.interpretation: Microglia activation persisted in the prefrontal cortex of mice that recovered from chronic colitis.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. The study successfully assessed neurobehavioural changes (reduced spontaneous activity) in a mouse model of extinguished chronic colitis and explored associated changes along the gut-brain axis (upregulation of neuroinflammation markers Lcn2, TNFa, S100A8, S100A9 and persistent microglia activation).
